old_claim.note: Mass-Produced Automobile provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped signal system used electric lights, switches, circuits, and a source of current to operate stop/move signals at intersections.

source_shape.source_locator: Patent specification describes electric signals, lights of different colors, circuit connections, a source of current, power mains, and electric control boxes for street traffic signals.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source makes electrical current, circuits, and lights core components of the municipal traffic-control system.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Automobile mass production explains traffic demand, but the source-backed mechanism of the scoped technology is electrical signal control at an intersection.

ontology_before: Traffic Signal Systems depended directly on automobile_mass_production, implying mass automobile manufacture enabled the signal mechanism.
ontology_after: Traffic Signal Systems depends directly on electricity for the electric lamps, circuits, power mains, and controls described in the patent.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: traffic_signal_systems now depends on electricity and no longer directly depends on automobile_mass_production.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped to automobile-era traffic-management demand rather than electric signal technology.
A stronger source shows the first traffic signal system was not electrically powered.
A separate electric_traffic_signals node is added and this node is broadened to non-electric traffic-control regimes.
short_reason: Electricity is the direct operating mechanism; mass automobile production is demand context.
